input.headline: Hurricane Melissa winds hit record-breaking 252 mph, data confirms

raw.headline: Hurricane Melissa winds hit record-breaking 252 mph, data confirms

winner: Why was Hurricane Melissa such a fast talker? Because her wind-er-speech hit record-breaking 252 mph!

candidates.text: Why did Hurricane Melissa refuse to join a band? Because she couldn't reach the high notes when she blew past 250 mph!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative pun on 'high notes' and wind speed, concise and specific to the headline."}

candidates.text: They say Hurricane Melissa reached a top wind speed of 252 mph. That's just her warm-up pace; she was late for an appointment.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific to the headline. It uses a relatable scenario (being late for an appointment) in a creative way, adding humor through personification of the hurricane."}

candidates.text: Why don't hurricanes ever join a book club? Because they can't handle record-breaking page speeds!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline with 'record-breaking,' but 'page speeds' is a weak and generic pun that lacks specificity and surprise."}

candidates.text: Why was Hurricane Melissa such a fast talker? Because her wind-er-speech hit record-breaking 252 mph!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke uses a play on words with 'wind-er-speech' but is slightly forced and lacks natural flow. It meets the headline's specifics but could be more surprising and concise."}

candidates.text: Why was Hurricane Melissa in a rush? Because she had 252 places to blow off steam!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline and uses wordplay on 'blow off steam,' but it's a bit generic and lacks stronger surprise or specificity tied to the hurricane's wind speed."}
